name.text: Using Similar Triangles to Find Unknown Side Lengths

questiontext.text:
Are triangle \(ABC\) and triangle \(PQR\) similar?

[[jsxgraph]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard('jxgbox', { boundingbox: [-2, 12, 16, -2], axis: false, pan: { enabled: false }, zoom: { enabled: false }, showNavigation: false }); /* === Triangle PQR (top-left) === */ var P = board.create('point', [1,10], {name:'', fixed:true, size:2}); var Q = board.create('point', [0,8], {name:'', fixed:true}); var R = board.create('point', [3,8], {name:'', fixed:true}); function edge(A,B){ return board.create('segment',[A,B],{strokeWidth:2,fixed:true}); } edge(P,Q); edge(Q,R); edge(R,P); // Vertex labels positioned neatly board.create('text',[P.X()-0.3, P.Y()+0.4, "P"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[Q.X()-0.4, Q.Y()-0.3, "Q"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[R.X()+0.2, R.Y()-0.3, "R"], {fixed:true}); // Side labels board.create('text',[()=>(P.X()+Q.X())/2-0.3, ()=>(P.Y()+Q.Y())/2+0.1, "{#PQ#} cm"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[()=>(P.X()+R.X())/2+0.2, ()=>(P.Y()+R.Y())/2+0.2, "{#PR#} cm"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[()=>(Q.X()+R.X())/2, ()=>(Q.Y()+R.Y())/2-0.4, "{#letter2#} cm"], {fixed:true}); // Fixed angle labels board.create('text',[P.X()-0.2, P.Y()-0.5, "55°"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[Q.X()+0.4, Q.Y()+0.3, "50°"], {fixed:true}); /* === Triangle ABC (bottom-right) === */ var A = board.create('point', [6,7], {name:'', fixed:true, size:2}); var B = board.create('point', [4.5,3], {name:'', fixed:true}); var C = board.create('point', [9,3], {name:'', fixed:true}); edge(A,B); edge(B,C); edge(C,A); // Vertex labels positioned neatly board.create('text',[A.X()-0.3, A.Y()+0.4, "A"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[B.X()-0.4, B.Y()-0.3, "B"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[C.X()+0.2, C.Y()-0.3, "C"], {fixed:true}); // Side labels board.create('text',[()=>(A.X()+B.X())/2-0.5, ()=>(A.Y()+B.Y())/2+0.5, "{#AB#} cm"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[()=>(A.X()+C.X())/2+0.2, ()=>(A.Y()+C.Y())/2+0.2, "{#letter1#} cm"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[()=>(B.X()+C.X())/2, ()=>(B.Y()+C.Y())/2-0.4, "{#BC#} cm"], {fixed:true}); // Fixed angle labels board.create('text',[A.X()-0.2, A.Y()-0.6, "55°"], {fixed:true}); board.create('text',[C.X()-0.6, C.Y()+0.2, "50°"], {fixed:true}); /* Prevent interaction */ board.renderer.svgRoot.style.pointerEvents = "none"; [[/jsxgraph]]

[[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]][[feedback:prt1]]

Hence find the values of {@letter2@}= [[input:ans2]] cm[[validation:ans2]] and {@letter1@} = [[input:ans3]] cm[[validation:ans3]]

.

[[feedback:prt2]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

\(PQ\) corresponds to \(AB\), \(PR\) corresponds to \(AC\) and \(QR\) corresponds to \(BC\).

\(\frac{AB}{PQ} = \frac{AC}{PR} = \frac{BC}{QR} = constant ratio\)

\(\angle QPR = \angle BAC = 55 ^\circ\)

\(\angle PRQ = \angle ACB = 50^\circ\)

\(\angle PQR = \angle ABC = 180^\circ-(75^\circ + 50^\circ)=50^\circ\)

\(\triangle\, PQR\) and \(\triangle \,ABC\) are similar

Thus, \(\frac{AB}{PQ} = \frac{AC}{PR} \Rightarrow \) \(\frac{{@AB@}}{{@PQ@}} = \frac{{@letter1@}}{{@PR@}}\)

\(\quad \quad \quad \quad \quad \quad {@letter1@}=\frac{{@AB@}\times {@PR@}}{{@PQ@}}={@AC@} \) cm

Similary, \(\frac{AB}{PQ} = \frac{BC}{QR} \Rightarrow \) \(\frac{{@AB@}}{{@PQ@}} = \frac{{@BC@}}{{@letter2@}}\)

\(\quad \quad \quad \quad \quad \quad{@letter2@}=\frac{{@PQ@}\times {@BC@}}{{@AB@}}={@QR@} \) cm

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

feedbackvariables.text: correst_ans : if (ans2 = ta2 and ans3 = ta1 ) then 1 else 0;

qtest.description: Test case assuming the teacher's input gets full marks.
